ssot_sources.shared_summary: Architecture Studio for API design, database design, design-pattern selection, and system design with black-box boundaries, concrete artifacts, and migration-safe recommendations.
shared_constraints: Publish architecture guidance only; do not orchestrate or delegate work.
Use deterministic, sectioned outputs with explicit scorecards.
Prefer replaceable interfaces and stable boundaries over implementation-heavy advice.
Make assumptions, trade-offs, evidence, and rollback explicit.
Validate and report migration impact when designs change interfaces or ownership.

modes.mode_summary: Design resource-oriented APIs with explicit resource models, endpoint contracts, validation, security, and error behavior.
uplift_notes: Prefer nouns and stable resource boundaries.
Keep auth, pagination, and versioning explicit.
Include at least one explicit rollback path for API changes.

modes.mode_summary: Design schemas and storage structures that match access patterns, consistency goals, and evolution needs.
uplift_notes: Call out intentional denormalization and failure impact.
Tie indexes to actual query paths.
Include consistency-vs-availability trade-off and rollback checkpoint.

modes.mode_summary: Choose structural patterns that reduce coupling and improve replaceability under changing requirements.
uplift_notes: Explain trade-offs and why each fit is chosen.
Avoid pattern cargo-culting; always include replacement implications.
Include rollback plan for each non-backward-compatible boundary change.

modes.mode_summary: Design end-to-end architecture with explicit scaling, reliability, observability, and migration plan.
uplift_notes: Separate policy from mechanism in each subsystem.

ssot_sources.display_name: Autosearch — Goal-Driven Improvement Search, Evaluation, and Promotion
ssot_sources.shared_summary: Autosearch for goal-oriented search, minimal-loop investigation, experiment design, repeated-trial evaluation, trace-to-regression distillation, and promotion-ready improvement loops across prompts, tools, systems, workflows, services, and code.
shared_constraints: Do not start search without a measurable goal.
Do not claim a winner from a single run unless the system is strictly deterministic and the user agrees.
Do not hide regressions inside one aggregate “score improved” claim.
Do not assume the host repo allows auto-commit or auto-merge.
Do not let the capability degrade into generic brainstorming or generic testing advice.
Do not confuse public inspiration with local proof.
Do not promote any candidate whose score is ambiguous, unstable, or unrepeatable.
Do not run broad candidate search when one sharply framed hypothesis can be tested first.
Do not parallelize dependent verification steps when regenerated artifacts are involved.

historical_baseline.reason: Repo-resident baseline source snapshot used as the fidelity oracle for future UAC judging.

tell me whether this candidate is good enough to promote
run experiments against a goal and tell me what actually wins
turn our failures into future eval cases
optimize this system without regressing quality
guide me from setup to experiments to commit and merge

shared_constraints: - forbidden: send, reply, forward, delete, archive, modify, or label any message during triage. Write commands (`+reply`, `+forward`, `+send`) are ONLY permitted during `/act` mode after explicit per-item user approval.
## Required Inputs
- `gws` CLI authenticated (`gws auth status` must pass)
## Required Output
Every pulse run must include:
### Example: Hot items only
Returns only 🔴 and 🟡 items
skipping 🔵 FYI noise.
| Safety invariant | No write commands executed without explicit per-item user approval |
Classify each item into 🔴 Urgent / 🟡 Needs Response / 🔵 FYI using deterministic rules.

forbidden: send, reply, forward, delete, archive, modify, or label any message during triage. Write commands (`+reply`, `+forward`, `+send`) are ONLY permitted during `/act` mode after explicit per-item user approval.

ssot_sources.display_name: Testing Studio — Test Design and Coverage Analysis
ssot_sources.shared_summary: Testing family for unit tests, end-to-end tests, edge-case discovery, and coverage gap analysis.
shared_constraints: Do not execute tests automatically.
Do not replace project-specific framework decisions when the repository already has a clear testing stack.
Do not claim coverage metrics without a coverage artifact or direct evidence.

modes.mode_summary: Generate isolated, framework-aware unit tests for functions, classes, and modules.
uplift_notes: Prefer readable assertions and deterministic setup.

modes.mode_summary: Design end-to-end tests around complete user or system journeys.
uplift_notes: Call out test-data setup and synchronization explicitly.
Prefer critical user paths over broad UI clicking.

modes.mode_summary: Identify boundary conditions, invalid states, and failure modes missing from current coverage.
uplift_notes: Rank by business or failure impact.

modes.mode_summary: Analyze current coverage and identify what remains untested or under-tested.
uplift_notes: Tie missing tests to specific code paths or workflows.
Separate critical gaps from routine improvements.

ssot_sources.display_name: Weekly Intelligence — Multi-Source Progress Report with Fact-Check Audit
ssot_sources.shared_summary: Multi-source weekly intelligence gathering, convergence, fact-check audit, and dual-report generation (technical + exec narrative) for any team, project, or time window.
shared_constraints: Do not modify any source system (tickets, repos, chat). Read only.
Do not assert a bug is "fixed" based on a single chat message without corroboration from the tracker or code.
Do not inflate ticket counts by including unrelated teams' work without disclosure.
Do not present created-after queries as merged-after without noting the caveat.
Do not skip the internal consistency check between body and appendices.
Do not use the word "impressive" or "amazing" in the main body. Let the numbers speak.
invocation_hints: what happened this week / last week / since Tuesday
weekly status report for my team
summarize progress across these chat spaces and repos
give me something I can share with leadership
pull together what we accomplished and what's at risk
